title: calchas bojeri
intro: Calchas bojeri, also known as the Stinking Passionflower, is a flowering vine native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with medicinal properties that include its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Calchas bojeri include its potential to alleviate anxiety and insomnia, as well as its antispasmodic and anti-arthritic effects. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas or infusions for medicinal purposes, and it is also sometimes used in traditional folk remedies and homeopathic treatments.
